item.title: My Summer In Germany
item.description:
I spent my entire summer in Germany, as an intern at Forschungszentrum Jülich through the DAAD RISE Program. It was a great experience all around, and I highly recommend anyone interested in spending some time abroad during his/her undergrad to consider this program, particularly over a typical study abroad.

  • Unlike a study abroad where you are paying tuition to attend, internships abroad are more often paid positions.
  • In my personal opinion, spending time abroad during the summer is better than during the academic year, since you’ll be missing out on less back home.

There were also things specific to my experience that made my time in Germany very worthwhile. I met wonderful peers, many in my same program, and mentors, whom I worked under. The lab I worked in was quite young, and mostly operated by Ph.D. students, all of whom were friendly and happy to teach me things. And indeed, I learned a lot. I’m very fortunate that I was entrusted with quite an independent research project – a first for me – and it gave me a great, authentic sense for what working in a wet lab, in a research group, and in a national laboratory are like.

item.title: About Me
item.description:
Hi! My name is Hugo.

I’m a third year undergraduate at UCLA, studying Materials Science and Engineering (with a focus on Electronic Materials). I’m also pursuing a technical breadth in Math/Computer Science. I have great intrest for materials research and am hoping to combine it with my growing skill set in software to solve complex problems. I’m honored to have received the Chin Family Endowed Engineering Scholarship and Knapp Scholarship for Ceramics Studies, which support my academic journey.

item.title: Sophienhöhe
item.description: I went on a hike in this manmade hill, made from the hole behind it! My new friend Denys and I came across hogs, deer, rabbits, and many other critters. It was a great day for sure and Sophienhöhe was only a brief bike ride away from home.

item.title: Bike Path to FZJ
item.description:
Here’s a nice shot of the bike path I used to take day to and from Forschungszentrum Jülich. Riding along this safe and beautiful path was a great start and end to each workday! I really loved bike-riding in Germany. The infrastructure to do so was spectacular.

Especially during my last few weeks in Jülich, I often sat at the bench hidden behind that middle tree to take in and appreciate my limited time left in Germany and to reflect on my summer. It was a nice routine after work.
